'''''Cloak of Deception''''' is a novel by [[James Luceno]] that takes place [[33 BBY/Legends|less than a year]] before [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']]. Narration for the audio version was performed by [[Alexander Adams]]. The paperback version included a 15-page excerpt of ''[[The New Jedi Order: Enemy Lines I: Rebel Dream]]''.

The novel and an [[Cloak of Deception (unabridged audiobook)|unabridged audiobook]] are set to be released by [[Random House Worlds]] on [[August 11]], [[2026]] as part of ''[[The Essential Legends Collection]]''.<ref name="Penguin-ELC" />

==Publisher's summary==
===Hardcover===
====Back cover====
One man's fall from power could lead to the end of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]], and the irreversible rise of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]]…

====Inside flap====
''Mired in greed and corruption, tangled in bureaucracy, the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] is crumbling. In the outlying [[Star system/Legends|systems]], where the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] maintains a stranglehold on shipping routes, tensions are boiling over—while back in the comfort of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], the hub of civilized space and seat of Republic government, few [[Senator/Legends|senators]] seem inclined to investigate the problem. And those who suspect [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Finis Valorum/Legends|Valorum]] of having a hand in the machinations are baffled—especially when [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] and his [[Padawan/Legends|apprentice]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] foil an assassination attempt on the Chancellor.''

''With the crisis escalating, Valorum calls for an [[Eriadu Trade Summit|emergency trade summit]]. As [[Human/Legends|Humans]] and [[Alien/Legends|aliens]] gather, conspiracies sealed with large sums of money run rampant, and no one is entirely above suspicion. But the greatest threat of all remains unknown to everyone except three members of the Trade Federation who have entered into a shadowy alliance with a [[Palpatine/Legends|dark overlord]]. While the trio will be content with more money and fewer problems, [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]] has grander, far more terrifying plans.''

''It is a time that tests the mettle of all those who strive to hold the Republic together—none more so than the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]], who have long been [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] best hope for preserving peace and justice. Yet despite their most valiant efforts, the meeting will explode into fiery chaos beyond everyone's worst fears…''

===Paperback===
''Mired in greed and corruption, tangled in bureaucracy, the Galactic Republic is crumbling. In the outlying systems, where the Trade Federation maintains a stranglehold on shipping routes, tensions are boiling over—and now even the comfort of Coruscant is being invaded, as Jedi Master Qui-Gon Jinn and his apprentice Obi-Wan Kenobi foil an assassination attempt on Supreme Chancellor Valorum.''

''As Humans and aliens gather for an emergency trade summit, conspiracies sealed with large sums of money run rampant, and no one is entirely above suspicion. But the greatest threat of all remains unknown to everyone except three members of the Trade Federation who have entered into a shadowy alliance with a dark overlord. While the trio will be content with more money and fewer problems, Darth Sidious has grander, far more terrifying plans.''

==Opening crawl==
{{OpeningCrawl
|name=CLOAK OF DECEPTION
|text=AFTER A THOUSAND GENERATIONS OF PEACE, THE
GALACTIC REPUBLIC IS CRUMBLING. ON CORUSCANT,
AT THE CENTER OF CIVILIZED SPACE, GREED AND
CORRUPTION RIDDLE THE SENATE, BEYOND EVEN
THE ABILITIES OF SUPREME CHANCELLOR VALORUM
TO REMEDY. AND IN THE OUTLYING SYSTEMS, THE
TRADE FEDERATION DOMINATES THE HYPERLANES
WITH ITS GARGANTUAN VESSELS.

BUT NOW EVEN THE TRADE FEDERATION FINDS
ITSELF ASSAILED FROM ALL QUARTERS, PREYED
UPON BY PIRATES AND RAIDERS, AND VICTIMIZED
BY TERRORISTS, WHO DEMAND AN END TO THE
FEDERATION'S TYRANNICAL PRACTICES.

IT IS A TIME THAT TESTS THE METTLE OF ALL
THOSE WHO STRIVE TO HOLD THE REPUBLIC
TOGETHER—NONE MORE THAN THE JEDI
KNIGHTS, WHO HAVE LONG BEEN THE
REPUBLIC'S BEST HOPE FOR PRESERVING PEACE
AND JUSTICE…}}

==Plot summary==
''Cloak of Deception'' is told in four parts: [[Dorvalla/Legends|Dorvalla]], Coruscant, The Outlying Systems, and The Inner Circle.

Shortly before the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Battle of Naboo]] ([[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]), [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]] is in the middle of his plans. As [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] Palpatine, he manipulates [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Finis Valorum/Legends|Valorum]] and other senators. As the [[Sith Master/Legends|Sith Master]], he begins to slowly put the [[Neimoidian/Legends|Neimoidians]] and the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] in position for his [[Blockade/Legends|blockade]] of [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]].

A terrorist group known as the [[Nebula Front/Legends|Nebula Front]] threatens the activities of the Trade Federation. They are protesting the actions of the group and will resort to any means necessary to disrupt the Trade Federation. They have hired [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Arwen Cohl]] to carry out [[Terrorist/Legends|terrorist]] acts against their business, including robbing a Trade Federation [[Starship/Legends|ship]] of [[Aurodium/Legends|aurodium]] ingots, worth billions in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dorvalla/Legends|Dorvalla]]. However, [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] are hot on the trail of the terrorists. Though Cohl escapes, he is forced to crash-land and all but him and two of his crew members, [[Rella]] and [[Boiny]], [[Death/Legends|perish]]. He later flees in his ship, the [[Hawk-Bat (gunship)|''Hawk-Bat'']].

Fearing more trouble, the Trade Federation petitions the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] to allow them to increase their number of [[Vulture-class starfighter/Legends|droid fighters]], [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|battle droids]], and other defenses. Valorum considers this, but only if he can tax some of the trade routes they hold (on advice from Palpatine). This sparks a debate and a summit on [[Eriadu/Legends|Eriadu]] is scheduled to be held on the matter.

Taking extreme measures, the Nebula Front sets plans in motion to assassinate Supreme Chancellor Valorum at the summit to prevent the taxation. Cohl has been hired by a mysterious being known only as [[Havac]]. He travels throughout the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]], hiring several experienced [[Assassin/Legends|assassins]]. Unknown to him however, Havac (secretly in league with Darth Sidious) has another plan. Meanwhile, the [[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]], along with Qui-Gon and Obi-Wan, steps in to track down Captain Cohl and the would-be assassins.

On Eriadu, Cohl dispatches his helpers throughout the [[Eriadu City/Legends|capital city]]. He is ambushed by Havac after realizing the true target. Most of the assassins are captured by the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] outside the summit building, but one remains inside. A [[Sniper/Legends|sniper]], he has been given the order to fire at, but miss, Valorum. The Jedi find Cohl and his partner Boiny, who, wounded, agrees to help them. They discover the sniper a second too late, but are surprised when he misses Valorum. Then Cohl remembers the true intent of Havac: to assassinate the [[Trade Federation Directorate]]. Cohl tackles Havac and kills him, though he and Boiny are mortally wounded in the attempt. Suddenly, the battle droids draw their [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]], influenced by the single [[Droid/Legends|droid]] present that didn't require a [[Central Control Computer/Legends|Central Control Computer]] to act. With a [[Deflector shield|shield activated]] for safety, the members of the Trade Federation are trapped. The droids kill them all while the crowd watches, stunned. The only survivors were the Neimoidian members, who were away to their ship which had been tampered with. In the ensuing shock, the bill to tax is passed by the Senate. Later, the leading members of the Federation are named. They are all Neimoidians. Darth Sidious later contacts [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray]] on the bridge of his ship, suggesting they invade [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] for revenge against Palpatine, who helped pass the bill. Little do they know that it is just part of the Sith Lord's plan for [[Galactic Empire/Legends|control]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].

==Development==
[[James Luceno]] was able to read the script of the unreleased [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']] while writing ''Cloak of Deception'' and was specifically asked by [[George Lucas]] to include one of the characters that would appear in the film. As the first ''Star Wars'' novel written after the completion of the ''Attack of the Clones'' script, ''Cloak of Deception'' was the first appearance of many characters and concepts that would make their on screen debut in the film,<ref name="Behind the Cloak">{{SWArchive|url=eu/lit/novel/f20010614/index.html|text=Behind the Cloak: James Luceno}}</ref> including the [[Techno Union/Legends|Techno Union]], [[Passel Argente/Legends|Passel Argente]], and other [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatists]].<ref name="Cloak of Deception">''Cloak of Deception''</ref>

Originally titled "Vergence", ''Cloak of Deception'' was the earliest adult novel in the timeline upon its publication. Since the events of [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''The Phantom Menace'']] occur after ''Deception'', Luceno compared [[Wikipedia:The Hobbit|''The Hobbit'''s]] relationship with [[Wikipedia:The Lord of the Rings|''The Lord of the Rings'' trilogy]] to ''Deception''<nowiki>'s relationship with the rest of the </nowiki>''Star Wars'' saga. Luceno made a conscious effort to fully introduce and explain the Jedi Order, the Galactic Republic, Lightsabers, the Galactic Senate and many other things.<ref name="TERC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref>

==Continuity==
[[James Luceno|Luceno’s]] rogue character [[Arwen Cohl]] was originally named Margrave M’buele in the outline, a nod to [[Katya M'buele]], a minor character from the [[Star Wars (1977)|Star Wars Marvel Comics]] run. The book had numerous notable cameo appearances. The [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Anoon Bondara]], [[Darsha Assant]], and the [[Neimoidia/Legends|Neimoidian]] [[Hath Monchar]] figured prominently in ''[[Darth Maul: Shadow Hunter]]'' ([[2001]]). Many of the commerce entities that helped fund the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist Alliance]] in [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|Episode II]] made an early appearance, as did the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luminara Unduli/Legends|Luminara Unduli]]. [[Jorus C'baoth]] appeared in one scene – he was a Jedi Master integral to the plot of [[Heir to the Empire|''Heir to the Empire'']] ([[1991]]) and [[Outbound Flight (novel)|''Outbound Flight'']] ([[2006]]).<ref name="TERC" />

One sly inclusion was [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Grebleips/Legends|Grebleips]] from [[Brodo Asogi]] – a reference to the [[Alien/Legends|aliens]] who resemble [https://ettheextraterrestrial.fandom.com/wiki/E.T._the_Extra-Terrestrial_(film)''E.T. The Extra Terrestrial''] in the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Senate]] scene in [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|Episode I]] ([[1999]]). Grebleips was [[Steven Spielberg|Spielberg]] [[Tuckerization|spelled backwards]], and Brodo Asogi was a name given to E.T.’s home [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in [[Wikipedia:E.T.: The Book of the Green Planet|''E.T.: The Book of the Green Planet'']], a spin-off novel to the movie ''E.T. The Extra Terrestrial''.<ref name="TERC" />
